title: Deep Dives
lede: Quantify the game on the fly. Each dive is an empirical answer built from thousands of WNBA games — read-only analysis, baked to static numbers, honest about its uncertainty.

dives.title: Even teams: scoreline odds by quarter
dives.one_line: Two evenly-matched teams — the odds of leading and winning at each quarter break.
dives.question: Even teams — odds of leading / winning at each quarter break?

dives.title: Comeback odds by deficit + time
dives.one_line: Trailing by N with T left — how often you come back to win.
dives.question: Down N with T left — win %?

dives.title: Home-court edge, decomposed
dives.one_line: Home is worth ~+2.1 pts / 55.5% — where does the edge actually come from?
dives.question: What is home worth, and why?

dives.title: Rest & back-to-back effects
dives.one_line: What a back-to-back costs a team, and whether stars get load-managed.
dives.question: How much does a rest edge / a B2B cost?

dives.title: Clutch: who rises and fades late
dives.one_line: Who shows up when it's close and late — the players and teams that rise or fade in the clutch.
dives.question: Who rises / fades in the clutch?

dives.title: Pace & possession value
dives.one_line: The fastest team runs ~6.9 more possessions a game than the slowest — but pace doesn't win; efficiency does.
dives.question: Does playing fast help you win?
